title: Own Your Power
summary: Rather than talking at teens about vaping and weed, we approached substance abuse through the lens of a mental health conversation.

body.text: As Executive Creative Director, I led my team in designing and fully producing the Own Your Power Oklahoma campaign for the Oklahoma Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services. The campaign was developed to empower teenagers to take control of their mental health and make healthier life choices without relying on vaping or smoking weed. By focusing on self agency, we encouraged teens to find inner strength and develop positive coping mechanisms instead of turning to substances for stress relief or relaxation.

The creative strategy centered on giving teens real tools and ownership over their well being. We created an engaging digital platform featuring practical healthy alternatives across categories such as creative outlets, physical activity, mindfulness practices, rest and relaxation techniques, and building support networks. Bold, youth centric messaging including Own Your Power, Time to self care not self medicate, and create your own high positioned teens as the heroes of their own stories, capable of finding happiness, peace, and relief from within.

The campaign effectively highlighted critical teen mental health challenges while promoting actionable solutions. It spotlighted key statistics such as one in three high school students experiencing persistent feelings of sadness or hopelessness, a 31 percent increase in teen mental health related visits during the pandemic, and that 75 percent of Oklahoma teens who recently vaped expressed a desire to reduce their use. By equipping young people with accessible resources and empowering them to make positive choices, Own Your Power became a meaningful step in preventing youth vaping and marijuana use across Oklahoma.
